1	import Foundation
2	
3	// MARK: - Credentials
4	
5	public struct CodexCredentials: Sendable, Equatable {
6	    public let accessToken: String
7	    public let accountId: AccountId
8	    public let accountEmail: AccountEmail?
9	    /// Parsed from `last_refresh` in auth.json; nil when the field is absent or unparseable.
10	    public let lastRefreshedAt: Date?
11	
12	    public init(
13	        accessToken: String,
14	        accountId: AccountId,
15	        accountEmail: AccountEmail? = nil,
16	        lastRefreshedAt: Date? = nil
17	    ) {
18	        self.accessToken = accessToken
19	        self.accountId = accountId
20	        self.accountEmail = accountEmail
21	        self.lastRefreshedAt = lastRefreshedAt
22	    }
23	
24	    public init(
25	        accessToken: String,
26	        accountId: String,
27	        accountEmail: AccountEmail? = nil,
28	        lastRefreshedAt: Date? = nil
29	    ) {
30	        self.init(
31	            accessToken: accessToken,
32	            accountId: AccountId(rawValue: accountId),
33	            accountEmail: accountEmail,
34	            lastRefreshedAt: lastRefreshedAt
35	        )
36	    }
37	}
38	
39	// MARK: - Protocol
40	
41	public protocol CodexAuthProviding: Sendable {
42	    func load() async throws -> CodexCredentials
43	}
44	
45	// MARK: - Error
46	
47	public enum CodexAuthError: Error, CustomStringConvertible {
48	    case noAuthFileFound(searchedPaths: [String])
49	    case readFailed(path: String, underlying: Error)
50	    case parseFailed(path: String, rawPreview: String)
51	    case missingAccountId(path: String)
52	    case keychainAccessDenied(exitCode: Int32)
53	    case keychainEmpty
54	    case keychainTimeout(timeoutSeconds: Int)
55	    case keychainParseFailed(rawPreview: String)
56	
57	    public var description: String {
58	        switch self {
59	        case let .noAuthFileFound(paths):
60	            "No Codex auth file found — searched: \(paths.joined(separator: ", "))"
61	        case let .readFailed(path, underlying):
62	            "Failed to read Codex auth file at '\(path)': \(underlying)"
63	        case let .parseFailed(path, preview):
64	            "Failed to parse Codex auth JSON at '\(path)' — preview: '\(preview.prefix(80))'"
65	        case let .missingAccountId(path):
66	            "Codex auth at '\(path)' has no tokens.account_id — cannot identify account"
67	        case let .keychainAccessDenied(code):
68	            "Keychain access denied for service 'Codex Auth' (exit \(code))"
69	        case .keychainEmpty:
70	            "Keychain item is empty for service 'Codex Auth'"
71	        case let .keychainTimeout(secs):
72	            "Keychain access timed out after \(secs)s for service 'Codex Auth'"
73	        case let .keychainParseFailed(preview):
74	            "Failed to parse Codex Auth keychain value — preview: '\(preview.prefix(80))'"
75	        }
76	    }
77	}
78	
79	// MARK: - Implementation
80	
81	public actor CodexAuth: CodexAuthProviding {
82	    private static let keychainService = "Codex Auth"
83	    private static let keychainTimeoutSeconds = 10
84	
85	    private let codexHomePath: String?
86	    private let fileManager: FileManager
87	    private let logger: FileLogger
88	    private let processRunner: ProcessRunning
89	
90	    // Actor-isolated; safe without nonisolated(unsafe)
91	    private let isoFormatter: ISO8601DateFormatter = {
92	        let f = ISO8601DateFormatter()
93	        f.formatOptions = [.withInternetDateTime, .withFractionalSeconds]
94	        return f
95	    }()
96	
97	    public init(
98	        codexHomePath: String? = nil,
99	        fileManager: FileManager = .default,
100	        logger: FileLogger = Loggers.codex,
101	        processRunner: ProcessRunning = FoundationProcessRunner()
102	    ) {
103	        self.codexHomePath = codexHomePath
104	        self.fileManager = fileManager
105	        self.logger = logger
106	        self.processRunner = processRunner
107	    }
108	
109	    public func load() async throws -> CodexCredentials {
110	        let home = fileManager.homeDirectoryForCurrentUser.path
111	
112	        // Build candidate paths in cascade order
113	        var candidates: [String] = []
114	        if let codexHome = codexHomePath ?? ProcessInfo.processInfo.environment["CODEX_HOME"] {
115	            candidates.append("\(codexHome)/auth.json")
116	        }
117	        candidates.append("\(home)/.config/codex/auth.json")
118	        candidates.append("\(home)/.codex/auth.json")
119	
120	        for path in candidates {
121	            guard fileManager.fileExists(atPath: path) else { continue }
122	            logger.log(.debug, "Loading Codex auth from \(path)")
123	            return try parseAuthFile(at: path)
124	        }
125	
126	        // Keychain fallback
127	        logger.log(.debug, "No auth.json found — falling back to Keychain '\(Self.keychainService)'")
128	        return try await loadFromKeychain()
129	    }
130	
131	    // MARK: - File parsing
132	
133	    private func parseAuthFile(at path: String) throws -> CodexCredentials {
134	        let data: Data
135	        do {
136	            data = try Data(contentsOf: URL(fileURLWithPath: path))
137	        } catch {
138	            throw CodexAuthError.readFailed(path: path, underlying: error)
139	        }
140	
141	        let jsonObject: Any
142	        do {
143	            jsonObject = try JSONSerialization.jsonObject(with: data)
144	        } catch {
145	            let preview = String(data: data.prefix(80), encoding: .utf8) ?? "<binary>"
146	            throw CodexAuthError.parseFailed(path: path, rawPreview: preview)
147	        }
148	        guard let json = jsonObject as? [String: Any] else {
149	            let preview = String(data: data.prefix(80), encoding: .utf8) ?? "<binary>"
150	            throw CodexAuthError.parseFailed(path: path, rawPreview: preview)
151	        }
152	
153	        let tokens = json["tokens"] as? [String: Any]
154	        guard let accessToken = tokens?["access_token"] as? String, !accessToken.isEmpty else {
155	            // Tolerate OPENAI_API_KEY-only auth files: no tokens dict means no account_id either
156	            let preview = String(data: data.prefix(80), encoding: .utf8) ?? "<binary>"
157	            throw CodexAuthError.parseFailed(path: path, rawPreview: preview)
158	        }
159	
160	        guard let accountId = tokens?["account_id"] as? String, !accountId.isEmpty else {
161	            throw CodexAuthError.missingAccountId(path: path)
162	        }
163	
164	        let lastRefreshedAt: Date?
165	        if let raw = json["last_refresh"] as? String {
166	            lastRefreshedAt = isoFormatter.date(from: raw)
167	        } else {
168	            lastRefreshedAt = nil
169	        }
170	
171	        return CodexCredentials(
172	            accessToken: accessToken,
173	            accountId: AccountId(rawValue: accountId),
174	            accountEmail: extractAccountEmail(from: tokens),
175	            lastRefreshedAt: lastRefreshedAt
176	        )
177	    }
178	
179	    // MARK: - Keychain
180	
181	    private func loadFromKeychain() async throws -> CodexCredentials {
182	        let serviceName = Self.keychainService
183	        let timeoutSecs = Self.keychainTimeoutSeconds
184	        let result = try await processRunner.run(
185	            executablePath: "/usr/bin/security",
186	            arguments: ["find-generic-password", "-s", serviceName, "-w"],
187	            timeoutSeconds: timeoutSecs
188	        )
189	        if result.timedOut {
190	            throw CodexAuthError.keychainTimeout(timeoutSeconds: timeoutSecs)
191	        }
192	        guard result.terminationStatus == 0 else {
193	            throw CodexAuthError.keychainAccessDenied(exitCode: result.terminationStatus)
194	        }
195	
196	        var rawString = String(data: result.stdout, encoding: .utf8)?.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines) ?? ""
197	        if rawString.isEmpty {
198	            throw CodexAuthError.keychainEmpty
199	        }
200	
201	        // Keychain may hex-encode the value with a leading "0x" prefix
202	        if rawString.hasPrefix("0x"), let decoded = Self.decodeHexUtf8(rawString) {
203	            rawString = decoded
204	        }
205	
206	        guard let jsonData = rawString.data(using: .utf8) else {
207	            throw CodexAuthError.keychainParseFailed(rawPreview: rawString)
208	        }
209	        let jsonObject: Any
210	        do {
211	            jsonObject = try JSONSerialization.jsonObject(with: jsonData)
212	        } catch {
213	            throw CodexAuthError.keychainParseFailed(rawPreview: rawString)
214	        }
215	        guard let json = jsonObject as? [String: Any],
216	              let tokens = json["tokens"] as? [String: Any],
217	              let accessToken = tokens["access_token"] as? String, !accessToken.isEmpty,
218	              let accountId = tokens["account_id"] as? String, !accountId.isEmpty else {
219	            throw CodexAuthError.keychainParseFailed(rawPreview: rawString)
220	        }
221	
222	        let lastRefreshedAt: Date?
223	        if let raw = json["last_refresh"] as? String {
224	            lastRefreshedAt = isoFormatter.date(from: raw)
225	        } else {
226	            lastRefreshedAt = nil
227	        }
228	
229	        return CodexCredentials(
230	            accessToken: accessToken,
231	            accountId: AccountId(rawValue: accountId),
232	            accountEmail: extractAccountEmail(from: tokens),
233	            lastRefreshedAt: lastRefreshedAt
234	        )
235	    }
236	
237	    private static func decodeHexUtf8(_ hex: String) -> String? {
238	        let hexBody = String(hex.dropFirst(2)) // drop "0x"
239	        guard hexBody.count % 2 == 0 else { return nil }
240	        var bytes: [UInt8] = []
241	        var index = hexBody.startIndex
242	        while index < hexBody.endIndex {
243	            let nextIndex = hexBody.index(index, offsetBy: 2)
244	            guard let byte = UInt8(hexBody[index..<nextIndex], radix: 16) else { return nil }
245	            bytes.append(byte)
246	            index = nextIndex
247	        }
248	        return String(bytes: bytes, encoding: .utf8)
249	    }
250	
251	    private func extractAccountEmail(from tokens: [String: Any]?) -> AccountEmail? {
252	        guard let tokens else { return nil }
253	        return extractAccountEmail(fromJWT: tokens["id_token"] as? String)
254	            ?? extractAccountEmail(fromJWT: tokens["access_token"] as? String)
255	    }
256	
257	    private func extractAccountEmail(fromJWT token: String?) -> AccountEmail? {
258	        guard let claims = Self.jwtClaims(from: token) else { return nil }
259	        return normalizedEmail(claims["email"] as? String)
260	            ?? normalizedEmail(claims["https://api.openai.com/profile.email"] as? String)
261	    }
262	
263	    private func normalizedEmail(_ rawValue: String?) -> AccountEmail? {
264	        guard let rawValue else { return nil }
265	        let trimmed = rawValue.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines)
266	        guard !trimmed.isEmpty else { return nil }
267	        return AccountEmail(rawValue: trimmed)
268	    }
269	
270	    private static func jwtClaims(from token: String?) -> [String: Any]? {
271	        guard let token else { return nil }
272	        let segments = token.split(separator: ".", omittingEmptySubsequences: false)
273	        guard segments.count >= 2,
274	              let payload = base64URLDecode(String(segments[1])),
275	              let object = try? JSONSerialization.jsonObject(with: payload),
276	              let claims = object as? [String: Any] else {
277	            return nil
278	        }
279	        return claims
280	    }
281	
282	    private static func base64URLDecode(_ input: String) -> Data? {
283	        let base64 = input
284	            .replacingOccurrences(of: "-", with: "+")
285	            .replacingOccurrences(of: "_", with: "/")
286	        let padding = (4 - (base64.count % 4)) % 4
287	        return Data(base64Encoded: base64 + String(repeating: "=", count: padding))
288	    }
289	}

1	import Foundation
2	
3	public actor ClaudeStatusConnector: StatusConnector {
4	    nonisolated public let vendor: Vendor = .claude
5	
6	    private let logger: FileLogger
7	    private let session: URLSession
8	    private let endpointURLString: String
9	
10	    private static let requestTimeoutSeconds: TimeInterval = 5
11	    public static let defaultEndpoint = "https://status.anthropic.com/api/v2/incidents/unresolved.json"
12	
13	    public init(
14	        logger: FileLogger = Loggers.claude,
15	        session: URLSession = .shared,
16	        endpointURLString: String = ClaudeStatusConnector.defaultEndpoint
17	    ) {
18	        self.logger = logger
19	        self.session = session
20	        self.endpointURLString = endpointURLString
21	    }
22	
23	    public func fetchOutages() async throws -> [Outage] {
24	        guard let url = URL(string: endpointURLString) else {
25	            throw StatusConnectorError.invalidURL(rawValue: endpointURLString)
26	        }
27	        var request = URLRequest(url: url)
28	        request.timeoutInterval = Self.requestTimeoutSeconds
29	        request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Accept")
30	
31	        logger.log(.debug, "Fetching Claude status from \(endpointURLString)")
32	
33	        let data: Data
34	        let response: URLResponse
35	        do {
36	            (data, response) = try await session.data(for: request)
37	        } catch {
38	            logger.log(.warning, "Status endpoint network error: \(error)")
39	            throw StatusConnectorError.networkError(underlying: error, url: endpointURLString)
40	        }
41	
42	        let httpCode = (response as? HTTPURLResponse)?.statusCode ?? -1
43	        guard httpCode == 200 else {
44	            logger.log(.warning, "Status endpoint returned HTTP \(httpCode)")
45	            throw StatusConnectorError.unexpectedResponse(statusCode: httpCode, url: endpointURLString)
46	        }
47	
48	        let decoded: StatuspageIncidentsResponse
49	        do {
50	            decoded = try JSONDecoder().decode(StatuspageIncidentsResponse.self, from: data)
51	        } catch {
52	            logger.log(.error, "Status endpoint parse error: \(error)")
53	            throw StatusConnectorError.parseError(underlying: error, url: endpointURLString)
54	        }
55	
56	        let outages: [Outage] = decoded.incidents.compactMap { incident in
57	            // "none" impact means the incident is informational with no user-facing effect —
58	            // don't surface it as an outage in the UI.
59	            guard incident.impact != "none" else { return nil }
60	            let severity = OutageSeverity(rawValue: incident.impact)
61	            // Invalid shortlink must not drop the incident itself — surface it without href.
62	            let href: URL?
63	            if let raw = incident.shortlink {
64	                href = URL(string: raw)
65	            } else {
66	                href = nil
67	            }
68	            return Outage(
69	                vendor: vendor,
70	                errorMessage: incident.name,
71	                severity: severity,
72	                since: ISODate(rawValue: incident.created_at),
73	                href: href
74	            )
75	        }
76	        logger.log(.info, "Claude status: \(outages.count) active outage(s)")
77	        return outages
78	    }
79	
80	    // MARK: - DTOs
81	
82	    private struct StatuspageIncidentsResponse: Decodable {
83	        let incidents: [StatuspageIncident]
84	    }
85	
86	    private struct StatuspageIncident: Decodable {
87	        let name: String
88	        let impact: String
89	        let created_at: String
90	        let shortlink: String?
91	    }
92	}

1	import SwiftUI
2	import AppKit
3	import AIUsagesTrackersLib
4	
5	enum VendorBranding {
6	    struct Brand: Equatable, Sendable {
7	        let assetName: String
8	        let displayName: String
9	        let tintHex: String
10	    }
11	
12	    static func brand(for vendor: Vendor) -> Brand? {
13	        switch vendor {
14	        case .claude:
15	            Brand(
16	                assetName: "claude-mark",
17	                displayName: "Claude Code",
18	                tintHex: "DA7756"
19	            )
20	        case .codex:
21	            Brand(
22	                assetName: "codex-mark",
23	                displayName: "Codex",
24	                tintHex: "10A37F"
25	            )
26	        case .copilot:
27	            Brand(
28	                assetName: "copilot-mark",
29	                displayName: "Copilot CLI",
30	                tintHex: "24292F"
31	            )
32	        default:
33	            nil
34	        }
35	    }
36	
37	    static func displayName(for vendor: Vendor) -> String {
38	        brand(for: vendor)?.displayName ?? vendor.rawValue
39	    }
40	
41	    static func tint(for vendor: Vendor) -> Color? {
42	        guard let tintHex = brand(for: vendor)?.tintHex else { return nil }
43	        return Color(hex: tintHex)
44	    }
45	
46	    static func icon(for vendor: Vendor) -> NSImage? {
47	        guard let assetName = brand(for: vendor)?.assetName else { return nil }
48	        return loadTemplateImage(named: assetName)
49	    }
50	
51	    /// Pixels of white halo added on each side around the vendor icon for legibility.
52	    static let iconGlowPadding: CGFloat = 3.0
53	
54	    /// Returns a tinted, non-template copy of the vendor icon at the requested height.
55	    /// Uses the brand's `tintHex` as fill color and the template PDF as an alpha mask.
56	    /// A crisp white halo is produced by drawing a white silhouette at pixel offsets
57	    /// in all directions — no blur, so the icon stays sharp on any menu bar tint color.
58	    @MainActor
59	    static func tintedNSImage(for vendor: Vendor, height: CGFloat) -> NSImage? {
60	        guard let template = icon(for: vendor),
61	              let brand = brand(for: vendor),
62	              let tintColor = NSColor(hex: brand.tintHex) else { return nil }
63	        let aspectRatio = template.size.height > 0
64	            ? template.size.width / template.size.height : 1
65	        let iconSize = NSSize(width: ceil(height * aspectRatio), height: ceil(height))
66	        let pad = Self.iconGlowPadding
67	        let canvasSize = NSSize(width: iconSize.width + pad * 2, height: iconSize.height + pad * 2)
68	        let iconRect = NSRect(x: pad, y: pad, width: iconSize.width, height: iconSize.height)
69	
70	        let tinted = NSImage(size: iconSize)
71	        tinted.lockFocus()
72	        tintColor.setFill()
73	        NSRect(origin: .zero, size: iconSize).fill()
74	        template.draw(
75	            in: NSRect(origin: .zero, size: iconSize),
76	            from: .zero,
77	            operation: .destinationIn,
78	            fraction: 1.0
79	        )
80	        tinted.unlockFocus()
81	
82	        let white = NSImage(size: iconSize)
83	        white.lockFocus()
84	        NSColor.white.setFill()
85	        NSRect(origin: .zero, size: iconSize).fill()
86	        template.draw(
87	            in: NSRect(origin: .zero, size: iconSize),
88	            from: .zero,
89	            operation: .destinationIn,
90	            fraction: 1.0
91	        )
92	        white.unlockFocus()
93	
94	        let result = NSImage(size: canvasSize)
95	        result.lockFocus()
96	        // Draw the white silhouette at every surrounding pixel offset to build a crisp halo.
97	        for dx: CGFloat in [-2, -1, 0, 1, 2] {
98	            for dy: CGFloat in [-2, -1, 0, 1, 2] where !(dx == 0 && dy == 0) {
99	                white.draw(in: NSRect(
100	                    x: iconRect.minX + dx, y: iconRect.minY + dy,
101	                    width: iconRect.width, height: iconRect.height
102	                ))
103	            }
104	        }
105	        tinted.draw(in: iconRect)
106	        result.unlockFocus()
107	        result.isTemplate = false
108	        return result
109	    }
110	
111	    /// Resolves the SwiftPM resource bundle without ever touching `Bundle.module`,
112	    /// whose synthesized accessor traps with a precondition failure when the
113	    /// expected `.bundle` is missing next to the executable. We probe every
114	    /// location the bundle may legitimately land in across `swift run`,
115	    /// `swift build`, the assembled `.app`, and merged-into-main-bundle layouts —
116	    /// returning `nil` instead of crashing the app when none match.
117	    private static let resourceBundleName = "AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers"
118	
119	    private static let resourceBundle: Bundle? = {
120	        let bundleFile = "\(resourceBundleName).bundle"
121	        let mainURL = Bundle.main.bundleURL
122	        let tokenBundle = Bundle(for: BundleToken.self)
123	        let candidates: [URL?] = [
124	            Bundle.main.resourceURL,
125	            mainURL,
126	            mainURL.appendingPathComponent("Contents/MacOS"),
127	            mainURL.appendingPathComponent("Contents/Resources"),
128	            tokenBundle.resourceURL,
129	            tokenBundle.bundleURL,
130	        ]
131	        for candidate in candidates.compactMap({ $0 }) {
132	            let bundleURL = candidate.appendingPathComponent(bundleFile)
133	            if let bundle = Bundle(url: bundleURL) {
134	                return bundle
135	            }
136	        }
137	        return nil
138	    }()
139	
140	    private static func loadTemplateImage(named assetName: String) -> NSImage? {
141	        let url = locateAsset(named: assetName, fileExtension: "pdf", subdirectory: "VendorBranding")
142	        guard let url, let image = NSImage(contentsOf: url) else { return nil }
143	        image.isTemplate = true
144	        return image
145	    }
146	
147	    private static func locateAsset(
148	        named assetName: String,
149	        fileExtension: String,
150	        subdirectory: String
151	    ) -> URL? {
152	        if let url = Bundle.main.url(
153	            forResource: assetName,
154	            withExtension: fileExtension,
155	            subdirectory: subdirectory
156	        ) {
157	            return url
158	        }
159	        if let url = resourceBundle?.url(
160	            forResource: assetName,
161	            withExtension: fileExtension,
162	            subdirectory: subdirectory
163	        ) {
164	            return url
165	        }
166	        return nil
167	    }
168	}
169	
170	private final class BundleToken {}
171	
172	struct VendorIconView: View {
173	    let vendor: Vendor
174	    var size: CGFloat = 14
175	
176	    var body: some View {
177	        if let image = VendorBranding.icon(for: vendor),
178	           let tint = VendorBranding.tint(for: vendor) {
179	            Image(nsImage: image)
180	                .renderingMode(.template)
181	                .resizable()
182	                .scaledToFit()
183	                .frame(width: size, height: size)
184	                .foregroundStyle(tint)
185	                .accessibilityHidden(true)
186	        } else {
187	            // Asset bundle missing (defensive fallback): show the vendor's first
188	            // letter so the rest of the UI stays informative instead of blank.
189	            Text(VendorBranding.displayName(for: vendor).prefix(1).uppercased())
190	                .font(.system(size: size * 0.8, weight: .semibold))
191	                .foregroundStyle(VendorBranding.tint(for: vendor) ?? .primary)
192	                .frame(width: size, height: size)
193	                .accessibilityHidden(true)
194	        }
195	    }
196	}
197	
198	struct VendorLabelView: View {
199	    let vendor: Vendor
200	    var iconSize: CGFloat = 14
201	
202	    var body: some View {
203	        HStack(spacing: 6) {
204	            VendorIconView(vendor: vendor, size: iconSize)
205	            Text(VendorBranding.displayName(for: vendor))
206	        }
207	    }
208	}
209	
210	private extension Color {
211	    init?(hex: String) {
212	        guard hex.count == 6, let value = Int(hex, radix: 16) else { return nil }
213	
214	        let red = Double((value >> 16) & 0xFF) / 255.0
215	        let green = Double((value >> 8) & 0xFF) / 255.0
216	        let blue = Double(value & 0xFF) / 255.0
217	
218	        self.init(red: red, green: green, blue: blue)
219	    }
220	}
221	
222	private extension NSColor {
223	    convenience init?(hex: String) {
224	        guard hex.count == 6, let value = Int(hex, radix: 16) else { return nil }
225	
226	        let red = CGFloat((value >> 16) & 0xFF) / 255.0
227	        let green = CGFloat((value >> 8) & 0xFF) / 255.0
228	        let blue = CGFloat(value & 0xFF) / 255.0
229	
230	        self.init(red: red, green: green, blue: blue, alpha: 1.0)
231	    }
232	}

1	import AppKit
2	import SwiftUI
3	import AIUsagesTrackersLib
4	import AppIconKit
5	
6	@MainActor
7	final class AppDelegate: NSObject, NSApplicationDelegate {
8	    private var poller: UsagePoller?
9	    private var snapshotScheduler: SnapshotScheduler?
10	    private var accountMonitor: ClaudeActiveAccountMonitor?
11	    private var codexMonitor: CodexActiveAccountMonitor?
12	    private var copilotMonitor: CopilotActiveAccountMonitor?
13	    private var pidGuard: AppPidGuard?
14	    private var usageStore: UsageStore?
15	    private var refreshState: RefreshState?
16	    private var statusItem: NSStatusItem?
17	    private var popover: NSPopover?
18	    private var appearanceObserver: NSKeyValueObservation?
19	    private var settingsWindowObserver: NSObjectProtocol?
20	    private var updateScheduler: UpdateScheduler?
21	    private var updateInstaller: UpdateInstaller?
22	    private var installationDetector: InstallationDetector?
23	
24	    /// Shared preferences store — exposed as static so the SwiftUI Settings scene
25	    /// (constructed before applicationDidFinishLaunching) can access it.
26	    static let sharedPreferences: UserDefaultsAppPreferences = UserDefaultsAppPreferences()
27	
28	    /// Shared usage store — populated in applicationDidFinishLaunching. The Settings
29	    /// scene is instantiated before the store exists, so it must read through this
30	    /// mutable holder. Nil until the first launch callback has run.
31	    static var sharedStore: UsageStore?
32	
33	    /// Shared observable update state — read by the popover banner and Settings.
34	    static let sharedUpdateState: UpdateState = UpdateState()
35	
36	    /// Pointer to the running update scheduler so Settings can trigger a manual check.
37	    static var sharedUpdateScheduler: UpdateScheduler?
38	
39	    /// Closure exposed to Settings so the user can launch installation of the
40	    /// current pending update without going through the popover banner.
41	    static var sharedTriggerUpdateInstall: (() -> Void)?
42	
43	    func applicationDidFinishLaunching(_ notification: Notification) {
44	        NSApplication.shared.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
45	
46	        // No .app bundle ships with this SwiftPM executable, so AppKit has no
47	        // static icon slot — set the Dock / Cmd+Tab icon programmatically.
48	        if let icon = AppIconRenderer.makeImage(pixelSize: 1024) {
49	            NSApplication.shared.applicationIconImage = icon
50	        }
51	
52	        let home = FileManager.default.homeDirectoryForCurrentUser.path
53	        let guard_ = AppPidGuard(cacheDir: "\(home)/.cache/ai-usages-tracker")
54	        do {
55	            try guard_.acquire()
56	        } catch AppPidGuardError.alreadyRunning(let pid, _) {
57	            let alert = NSAlert()
58	            alert.messageText = "AI Usages Tracker is already running"
59	            alert.informativeText = "Another instance is active (PID \(pid)). Use the menu bar icon to quit it before launching a new one."
60	            alert.runModal()
61	            NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
62	            return
63	        } catch {
64	            let alert = NSAlert()
65	            alert.messageText = "Failed to start AI Usages Tracker"
66	            alert.informativeText = error.localizedDescription
67	            alert.runModal()
68	            NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
69	            return
70	        }
71	        pidGuard = guard_
72	
73	        Loggers.setPreferences(Self.sharedPreferences)
74	
75	        MenuBarSegmentsSeeder.seedIfNeeded(preferences: Self.sharedPreferences)
76	        ChartConfigurationsSeeder.seedIfNeeded(preferences: Self.sharedPreferences)
77	
78	        // Reconcile launch-at-login preference with system state — the user may
79	        // have disabled the entry in System Settings > Login Items directly.
80	        let launchService = LaunchAtLoginService.shared
81	        if Self.sharedPreferences.launchAtLogin != launchService.isEnabled {
82	            Self.sharedPreferences.launchAtLogin = launchService.isEnabled
83	        }
84	
85	        let fileManager = UsagesFileManager.shared
86	        let refreshState = RefreshState()
87	        let codexConnector = CodexConnector()
88	        let copilotConnector = CopilotConnector()
89	        let poller = UsagePoller(
90	            connectors: [ClaudeCodeConnector(), codexConnector, copilotConnector],
91	            statusConnectors: [ClaudeStatusConnector(), CodexStatusConnector()],
92	            fileManager: fileManager,
93	            refreshState: refreshState,
94	            preferences: Self.sharedPreferences
95	        )
96	        let accountMonitor = ClaudeActiveAccountMonitor(
97	            fileManager: fileManager,
98	            onActiveAccountChanged: { [weak poller] _ in
99	                await poller?.pollOnce(force: true)
100	            }
101	        )
102	        let codexMonitor = CodexActiveAccountMonitor(
103	            onActiveAccountChanged: { [weak codexConnector, weak poller] _ in
104	                await codexConnector?.invalidateEmailCache()
105	                await poller?.pollOnce(force: true)
106	            }
107	        )
108	        let copilotMonitor = CopilotActiveAccountMonitor(
109	            onActiveAccountChanged: { [weak copilotConnector, weak poller] _ in
110	                await copilotConnector?.invalidateLoginCache()
111	                await poller?.pollOnce(force: true)
112	            }
113	        )
114	        let snapshotRecorder = SnapshotRecorder()
115	        let historyReader = UsageHistoryReader(rootPath: snapshotRecorder.rootPath)
116	        let snapshotScheduler = SnapshotScheduler(
117	            fileManager: fileManager,
118	            recorder: snapshotRecorder
119	        )
120	        self.poller = poller
121	        self.snapshotScheduler = snapshotScheduler
122	        self.accountMonitor = accountMonitor
123	        self.codexMonitor = codexMonitor
124	        self.copilotMonitor = copilotMonitor
125	        self.refreshState = refreshState
126	
127	        let usagesPath = fileManager.filePath
128	        let fileWatcher = UsagesFileWatcher(path: usagesPath)
129	        let store = UsageStore(fileWatcher: fileWatcher, preferences: Self.sharedPreferences)
130	        self.usageStore = store
131	        Self.sharedStore = store
132	
133	        Self.sharedUpdateState.dismissedVersions = Set(Self.sharedPreferences.updatesDismissedVersions)
134	
135	        setupStatusItem(store: store, refreshState: refreshState, historyReader: historyReader)
136	        trackStoreChanges(store: store)
137	        setupUpdateScheduler()
138	
139	        Task {
140	            await StartupMigrationRunner(fileManager: fileManager).run()
141	            store.start()
142	            await poller.start()
143	            await snapshotScheduler.start()
144	            await accountMonitor.start()
145	            await codexMonitor.start()
146	            await copilotMonitor.start()
147	            if let scheduler = self.updateScheduler {
148	                await scheduler.start()
149	            }
150	        }
151	    }
152	
153	    private func setupUpdateScheduler() {
154	        // In a development build (swift run) there's no Info.plist version, so
155	        // we fall back to 0.0.0 — every release will then be reported as newer,
156	        // but the existing "Skip this version" flow ensures the alert isn't
157	        // re-shown until a different version ships.
158	        let effectiveVersion = Self.currentAppVersion() ?? AppVersion(string: "0.0.0")!
159	        let bundlePath = Bundle.main.bundleURL.path
160	        let detector = InstallationDetector(bundlePath: bundlePath)
161	        self.installationDetector = detector
162	        self.updateInstaller = UpdateInstaller()
163	        let scheduler = UpdateScheduler(
164	            checker: UpdateChecker(),
165	            detector: detector,
166	            currentVersion: effectiveVersion,
167	            preferencesAccessor: { Self.sharedPreferences },
168	            stateAccessor: { Self.sharedUpdateState },
169	            onUpdateAvailable: { [weak self] update, kind in
170	                self?.presentUpdateAlert(update: update, kind: kind)
171	            }
172	        )
173	        self.updateScheduler = scheduler
174	        Self.sharedUpdateScheduler = scheduler
175	        Self.sharedTriggerUpdateInstall = { [weak self] in
176	            self?.triggerUpdateInstall()
177	        }
178	    }
179	
180	    static func currentAppVersion() -> AppVersion? {
181	        guard let raw = Bundle.main.object(forInfoDictionaryKey: "CFBundleShortVersionString") as? String else {
182	            return nil
183	        }
184	        return AppVersion(string: raw)
185	    }
186	
187	    /// Human-readable label for the running app version, falling back to a
188	    /// "development build" marker when launched via `swift run` (no Info.plist).
189	    static func currentAppVersionLabel() -> String {
190	        currentAppVersion()?.rawValue ?? "development build"
191	    }
192	
193	    func applicationWillTerminate(_ notification: Notification) {
194	        quit()
195	    }
196	
197	    // MARK: - Status item
198	
199	    private func setupStatusItem(
200	        store: UsageStore,
201	        refreshState: RefreshState,
202	        historyReader: UsageHistoryReader
203	    ) {
204	        let item = NSStatusBar.system.statusItem(withLength: NSStatusItem.variableLength)
205	        statusItem = item
206	
207	        let popover = NSPopover()
208	        popover.behavior = .transient
209	        let hosting = NSHostingController(
210	            rootView: UsageDetailsView(
211	                store: store,
212	                refreshState: refreshState,
213	                historyReader: historyReader,
214	                updateState: Self.sharedUpdateState,
215	                onRefresh: { [weak self] in
216	                    await self?.poller?.pollOnce(now: Date(), force: true)
217	                },
218	                onOpenSettings: { [weak self] in
219	                    self?.openSettings()
220	                },
221	                onQuit: { [weak self] in
222	                    self?.quit()
223	                },
224	                onInstallUpdate: { [weak self] in
225	                    self?.triggerUpdateInstall()
226	                },
227	                onSkipUpdate: { [weak self] in
228	                    self?.skipCurrentUpdate()
229	                },
230	                onLaterUpdate: { [weak self] in
231	                    self?.laterUpdate()
232	                }
233	            )
234	        )
235	        // Let SwiftUI's intrinsic size drive the popover so it grows with content
236	        // up to the screen-ratio cap enforced inside UsageDetailsView.
237	        hosting.sizingOptions = [.preferredContentSize]
238	        popover.contentViewController = hosting
239	        self.popover = popover
240	
241	        if let button = item.button {
242	            button.target = self
243	            button.action = #selector(togglePopover(_:))
244	            // The menu bar appearance can change (wallpaper-driven tinting) without
245	            // the app's effectiveAppearance changing, so we observe the button itself.
246	            appearanceObserver = button.observe(\.effectiveAppearance, options: [.new]) { [weak self] _, _ in
247	                Task { @MainActor in self?.refreshStatusItemImage() }
248	            }
249	        }
250	
251	        refreshStatusItemImage()
252	    }
253	
254	    private func refreshStatusItemImage() {
255	        guard let store = usageStore, let button = statusItem?.button else { return }
256	        let isDark = button.effectiveAppearance.bestMatch(from: [.darkAqua, .aqua]) == .darkAqua
257	        let isUnconfigured = Self.sharedPreferences.menuBarSegments.isEmpty
258	        let warningPrefix = outageWarningPrefix(store: store)
259	        let image = MenuBarLabelRenderer.render(
260	            segments: store.menuBarSegments,
261	            separator: Self.sharedPreferences.menuBarSeparator,
262	            fallbackText: store.menuBarText,
263	            isDarkMenuBar: isDark,
264	            isUnconfigured: isUnconfigured,
265	            outageWarningPrefix: warningPrefix
266	        )
267	        button.image = image
268	    }
269	
270	    /// Returns the configured warning text when outage hinting is enabled and at
271	    /// least one vendor currently has an active outage; nil otherwise.
272	    private func outageWarningPrefix(store: UsageStore) -> String? {
273	        let prefs = Self.sharedPreferences
274	        guard prefs.menuBarOutageWarningEnabled else { return nil }
275	        let text = prefs.menuBarOutageWarningText
276	        guard !text.isEmpty else { return nil }
277	        guard store.outagesByVendor.values.contains(where: { !$0.isEmpty }) else { return nil }
278	        return text
279	    }
280	
281	    @objc private func togglePopover(_ sender: Any?) {
282	        guard let popover, let button = statusItem?.button else { return }
283	        if popover.isShown {
284	            popover.performClose(sender)
285	        } else {
286	            popover.show(relativeTo: button.bounds, of: button, preferredEdge: .minY)
287	            popover.contentViewController?.view.window?.makeKey()
288	        }
289	    }
290	
291	    // MARK: - Observation
292	
293	    /// Re-arms `withObservationTracking` after every change so the status item
294	    /// keeps mirroring the store. Tracking fires exactly once per registration.
295	    private func trackStoreChanges(store: UsageStore) {
296	        let prefs = Self.sharedPreferences
297	        withObservationTracking {
298	            _ = store.menuBarSegments
299	            _ = store.menuBarText
300	            _ = store.outagesByVendor
301	            _ = prefs.menuBarOutageWarningEnabled
302	            _ = prefs.menuBarOutageWarningText
303	        } onChange: { [weak self] in
304	            Task { @MainActor in
305	                guard let self, let store = self.usageStore else { return }
306	                self.refreshStatusItemImage()
307	                self.trackStoreChanges(store: store)
308	            }
309	        }
310	    }
311	
312	    // MARK: - Settings
313	
314	    private func openSettings() {
315	        popover?.performClose(nil)
316	        // .accessory apps have no dock presence; showSettingsWindow: silently fails
317	        // unless we temporarily switch to .regular so AppKit can make the window key.
318	        NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.regular)
319	        NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
320	
321	        // SwiftUI creates the Settings window lazily on first showSettingsWindow: —
322	        // if it already exists, reuse it directly to avoid responder-chain races
323	        // that occur during the popover-dismissal + activation-policy transition.
324	        if let settingsWindow = Self.findSettingsWindow() {
325	            settingsWindow.makeKeyAndOrderFront(nil)
326	        } else {
327	            // First-time path: AppKit needs a runloop tick after the .accessory →
328	            // .regular transition to install the standard App menu (which is where
329	            // SwiftUI wires the Settings… action). sendAction with a nil target
330	            // relies on that wiring, so we trigger the menu item directly once
331	            // AppKit has had a chance to build it.
332	            DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 0.15) {
333	                Self.triggerSettingsMenuItem()
334	                if let settingsWindow = Self.findSettingsWindow() {
335	                    settingsWindow.makeKeyAndOrderFront(nil)
336	                }
337	            }
338	        }
339	
340	        // Restore .accessory policy once the settings window is closed.
341	        if settingsWindowObserver == nil {
342	            // Use selector-based observer so the callback runs on @MainActor naturally,
343	            // avoiding Sendable-crossing issues with block-based addObserver(queue:).
344	            settingsWindowObserver = self
345	            NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
346	                self,
347	                selector: #selector(handleSettingsWindowWillClose(_:)),
348	                name: NSWindow.willCloseNotification,
349	                object: nil
350	            )
351	        }
352	    }
353	
354	    @objc private func handleSettingsWindowWillClose(_ notification: Notification) {
355	        // Only react to the Settings window closing — popovers and other
356	        // transient windows also emit willClose.
357	        guard let closingWindow = notification.object as? NSWindow else { return }
358	        let windowIdentifier = closingWindow.identifier?.rawValue ?? ""
359	        guard windowIdentifier.contains("Settings") || windowIdentifier.contains("Preferences") else { return }
360	        // willClose fires while the window is still visible and before
361	        // AppKit tears it down; defer to the next runloop tick so the
362	        // activation-policy change isn't overridden by pending AppKit work.
363	        DispatchQueue.main.async { [weak self] in
364	            NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
365	            // Accessory transition doesn't always remove the app from the
366	            // Cmd+Tab / app-switcher list until another app is activated.
367	            if let frontmost = NSWorkspace.shared.runningApplications.first(where: {
368	                $0.isActive && $0.processIdentifier != ProcessInfo.processInfo.processIdentifier
369	            }) {
370	                frontmost.activate()
371	            } else {
372	                NSApp.hide(nil)
373	            }
374	            guard let self else { return }
375	            NotificationCenter.default.removeObserver(self, name: NSWindow.willCloseNotification, object: nil)
376	            self.settingsWindowObserver = nil
377	        }
378	    }
379	
380	    /// SwiftUI tags its Settings window with an identifier containing "Settings"
381	    /// (or "Preferences" on older SDKs). Match on the stable identifier rather
382	    /// than localized titles.
383	    private static func findSettingsWindow() -> NSWindow? {
384	        NSApp.windows.first { window in
385	            let identifier = window.identifier?.rawValue ?? ""
386	            return identifier.contains("Settings") || identifier.contains("Preferences")
387	        }
388	    }
389	
390	    /// Walk the main menu looking for the App menu's Settings… (or Preferences…)
391	    /// item and invoke it. Going through the menu guarantees the action is
392	    /// routed to whichever responder SwiftUI wired up, instead of relying on
393	    /// `sendAction(_:to:from:)` to resolve a nil target in the responder chain
394	    /// — which is unreliable right after an activation-policy switch.
395	    private static func triggerSettingsMenuItem() {
396	        guard let mainMenu = NSApp.mainMenu else { return }
397	        for topItem in mainMenu.items {
398	            guard let submenu = topItem.submenu else { continue }
399	            for (index, item) in submenu.items.enumerated() {
400	                let title = item.title
401	                if title.hasPrefix("Settings") || title.hasPrefix("Preferences") {
402	                    submenu.performActionForItem(at: index)
403	                    return
404	                }
405	            }
406	        }
407	    }
408	
409	    // MARK: - Updates
410	
411	    private func presentUpdateAlert(update: AvailableUpdate, kind: InstallationKind) {
412	        let alert = NSAlert()
413	        alert.icon = NSApplication.shared.applicationIconImage
414	        alert.messageText = "Update available"
415	        alert.informativeText = "Version \(update.version.rawValue) of AI Usages Tracker is available. Install it now?"
416	        alert.alertStyle = .informational
417	        let installLabel = kind == .homebrewCask ? "Update via Homebrew" : "Install"
418	        alert.addButton(withTitle: installLabel)
419	        alert.addButton(withTitle: "Later")
420	        alert.addButton(withTitle: "Skip this version")
421	        // .accessory apps don't get focus by default; activate so the modal alert
422	        // surfaces above other windows when the user is mid-task.
423	        let previousPolicy = NSApp.activationPolicy()
424	        NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.regular)
425	        NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
426	        defer { NSApp.setActivationPolicy(previousPolicy) }
427	
428	        switch alert.runModal() {
429	        case .alertFirstButtonReturn:
430	            triggerUpdateInstall()
431	        case .alertSecondButtonReturn:
432	            break
433	        case .alertThirdButtonReturn:
434	            skipCurrentUpdate()
435	        default:
436	            break
437	        }
438	    }
439	
440	    private func triggerUpdateInstall() {
441	        guard let installer = updateInstaller,
442	              let detector = installationDetector,
443	              let update = Self.sharedUpdateState.availableUpdate else {
444	            return
445	        }
446	        let kind = Self.sharedUpdateState.installationKind ?? .manual
447	        let runningPath = Bundle.main.bundleURL.path
448	        // For manual installs, the script needs an actual `.app` bundle path —
449	        // fine when running from /Applications/Foo.app, but `swift run` or any
450	        // ad-hoc binary lives in a flat directory. Prompt the user for an
451	        // installation directory and synthesize the target `.app` path from it.
452	        let bundlePath: String
453	        if kind == .manual && !runningPath.hasSuffix(".app") {
454	            guard let chosen = Self.promptForInstallDirectory(startingAt: runningPath) else {
455	                return
456	            }
457	            bundlePath = (chosen as NSString).appendingPathComponent("\(Self.appBundleDisplayName).app")
458	        } else {
459	            bundlePath = runningPath
460	        }
461	        Self.sharedUpdateState.setInstalling()
462	        let installation = InstallationInfo(
463	            kind: kind,
464	            bundlePath: bundlePath
465	        )
466	        let pid = ProcessInfo.processInfo.processIdentifier
467	        Task { [weak self] in
468	            do {
469	                let brewPath = await detector.brewExecutablePath()
470	                let plan = try await installer.buildPlan(
471	                    for: update,
472	                    installation: installation,
473	                    brewExecutablePath: brewPath,
474	                    currentPID: pid
475	                )
476	                if plan.requiresAdminPrivileges {
477	                    let confirmed = await MainActor.run { Self.confirmAdminElevation(version: update.version.rawValue) }
478	                    guard confirmed else {
479	                        await MainActor.run { Self.sharedUpdateState.setIdle(checkedAt: Date()) }
480	                        return
481	                    }
482	                    try await Self.launchWithAdminPrivileges(scriptPath: plan.scriptPath)
483	                } else {
484	                    try Self.launchDetached(scriptPath: plan.scriptPath)
485	                }
486	                await MainActor.run {
487	                    self?.quit()
488	                }
489	            } catch {
490	                await MainActor.run {
491	                    let alert = NSAlert()
492	                    alert.icon = NSApplication.shared.applicationIconImage
493	                    alert.messageText = "Failed to install update"
494	                    alert.informativeText = String(describing: error)
495	                    alert.runModal()
496	                    Self.sharedUpdateState.setError(String(describing: error), at: Date())
497	                }
498	            }
499	        }
500	    }
501	
502	    /// Display name used to synthesize the install target when running from a
503	     /// non-`.app` location (e.g. `swift run`). Mirrors `scripts/build-app-bundle.sh`.
504	    private static let appBundleDisplayName = "AI Usages Tracker"
505	
506	    /// Prompts the user for the directory in which to install the app. Returns
507	    /// nil if the user cancels. Defaults to the parent directory of the current
508	    /// binary, falling back to `/Applications` when that's a build artifact dir.
509	    @MainActor
510	    private static func promptForInstallDirectory(startingAt currentPath: String) -> String? {
511	        let panel = NSOpenPanel()
512	        panel.canChooseFiles = false
513	        panel.canChooseDirectories = true
514	        panel.canCreateDirectories = true
515	        panel.allowsMultipleSelection = false
516	        panel.title = "Select installation directory"
517	        panel.message = "Choose where AI Usages Tracker should be installed."
518	        panel.prompt = "Install here"
519	        let parent = (currentPath as NSString).deletingLastPathComponent
520	        let defaultURL: URL
521	        if parent.contains("/.build/") || parent.hasSuffix("/.build") {
522	            defaultURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: "/Applications")
523	        } else {
524	            defaultURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: parent)
525	        }
526	        panel.directoryURL = defaultURL
527	        let previousPolicy = NSApp.activationPolicy()
528	        NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.regular)
529	        NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
530	        defer { NSApp.setActivationPolicy(previousPolicy) }
531	        guard panel.runModal() == .OK else { return nil }
532	        return panel.url?.path
533	    }
534	
535	    @MainActor
536	    private static func confirmAdminElevation(version: String) -> Bool {
537	        let alert = NSAlert()
538	        alert.icon = NSApplication.shared.applicationIconImage
539	        alert.messageText = "Administrator permission required"
540	        alert.informativeText = "AI Usages Tracker is installed in a location that requires administrator privileges to update. macOS will prompt you for your password to install version \(version)."
541	        alert.alertStyle = .warning
542	        alert.addButton(withTitle: "Continue")
543	        alert.addButton(withTitle: "Cancel")
544	        let previousPolicy = NSApp.activationPolicy()
545	        NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.regular)
546	        NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
547	        defer { NSApp.setActivationPolicy(previousPolicy) }
548	        return alert.runModal() == .alertFirstButtonReturn
549	    }
550	
551	    /// Launch the install script as root via AppleScript's
552	    /// `do shell script ... with administrator privileges`. The bash script is
553	    /// spawned in the background (`&`) so osascript exits as soon as the user
554	    /// approves the prompt — the script itself waits for the parent app to
555	    /// terminate before performing the actual swap.
556	    private static func launchWithAdminPrivileges(scriptPath: String) async throws {
557	        let escaped = scriptPath
558	            .replacingOccurrences(of: "\\", with: "\\\\")
559	            .replacingOccurrences(of: "\"", with: "\\\"")
560	        let appleScript = """
561	        do shell script "/bin/bash \\"\(escaped)\\" >/dev/null 2>&1 &" with administrator privileges
562	        """
563	        try await withCheckedThrowingContinuation { (continuation: CheckedContinuation<Void, Error>) in
564	            DispatchQueue.global().async {
565	                let process = Process()
566	                process.executableURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: "/usr/bin/osascript")
567	                process.arguments = ["-e", appleScript]
568	                process.standardOutput = FileHandle.nullDevice
569	                process.standardError = FileHandle.nullDevice
570	                do {
571	                    try process.run()
572	                } catch {
573	                    continuation.resume(throwing: error)
574	                    return
575	                }
576	                process.waitUntilExit()
577	                guard process.terminationStatus == 0 else {
578	                    continuation.resume(throwing: NSError(
579	                        domain: "AIUsagesTracker.UpdateInstaller",
580	                        code: Int(process.terminationStatus),
581	                        userInfo: [NSLocalizedDescriptionKey: "Authorization was cancelled or denied (status \(process.terminationStatus))."]
582	                    ))
583	                    return
584	                }
585	                continuation.resume()
586	            }
587	        }
588	    }
589	
590	    private func skipCurrentUpdate() {
591	        guard let version = Self.sharedUpdateState.availableUpdate?.version.rawValue else { return }
592	        Self.sharedUpdateState.dismissCurrent()
593	        var stored = Self.sharedPreferences.updatesDismissedVersions
594	        if !stored.contains(version) {
595	            stored.append(version)
596	            Self.sharedPreferences.updatesDismissedVersions = stored
597	        }
598	    }
599	
600	    private func laterUpdate() {
601	        // No-op aside from clearing the in-popover banner this session.
602	        Self.sharedUpdateState.dismissCurrent()
603	    }
604	
605	    private static func launchDetached(scriptPath: String) throws {
606	        let process = Process()
607	        process.executableURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: "/bin/bash")
608	        process.arguments = [scriptPath]
609	        // Detach: redirect IO to /dev/null so the child outlives the app.
610	        process.standardOutput = FileHandle.nullDevice
611	        process.standardError = FileHandle.nullDevice
612	        process.standardInput = FileHandle.nullDevice
613	        try process.run()
614	    }
615	
616	    // MARK: - Quit
617	
618	    private func quit() {
619	        let pollerRef = poller
620	        let schedulerRef = snapshotScheduler
621	        let monitorRef = accountMonitor
622	        let codexMonitorRef = codexMonitor
623	        let copilotMonitorRef = copilotMonitor
624	        Task {
625	            await pollerRef?.stop()
626	            await schedulerRef?.stop()
627	            await monitorRef?.stop()
628	            await codexMonitorRef?.stop()
629	            await copilotMonitorRef?.stop()
630	        }
631	        usageStore?.stop()
632	        pidGuard?.release()
633	        NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
634	    }
635	}

// swift-tools-version:6.0
import PackageDescription

let package = Package(
    name: "AIUsagesTrackers",
    platforms: [.macOS(.v14)],
    dependencies: [
        .package(url: "https://github.com/SimplyDanny/SwiftLintPlugins", from: "0.57.0"),
    ],
    targets: [
        .target(
            name: "AIUsagesTrackersLib",
            path: "Sources/AIUsagesTrackers",
            plugins: [.plugin(name: "SwiftLintBuildToolPlugin", package: "SwiftLintPlugins")]
        ),
        .target(
            name: "AppIconKit",
            path: "Sources/AppIconKit",
            plugins: [.plugin(name: "SwiftLintBuildToolPlugin", package: "SwiftLintPlugins")]
        ),
        .executableTarget(
            name: "AIUsagesTrackers",
            dependencies: ["AIUsagesTrackersLib", "AppIconKit"],
            path: "Sources/App",
            resources: [.copy("Resources/VendorBranding")],
            plugins: [.plugin(name: "SwiftLintBuildToolPlugin", package: "SwiftLintPlugins")]
        ),
        .executableTarget(
            name: "IconExporter",
            dependencies: ["AppIconKit"],
            path: "Sources/IconExporter",
            plugins: [.plugin(name: "SwiftLintBuildToolPlugin", package: "SwiftLintPlugins")]
        ),
        .testTarget(
            name: "AIUsagesTrackersTests",
            dependencies: ["AIUsagesTrackersLib", "AIUsagesTrackers"],
            path: "Tests/AIUsagesTrackersTests",
            plugins: [.plugin(name: "SwiftLintBuildToolPlugin", package: "SwiftLintPlugins")]
        ),
    ]
)

# SwiftLint configuration for AIUsagesTrackers.
#
# Rule codes mirror .claude/rules/swift-quality.md — keep the mapping in sync.
# Severities: `error` fails the build; `warning` surfaces in logs / IDE.

included:
  - Sources
  - Tests

# Narrow the SwiftLint surface to exactly the codes documented in
# .claude/rules/swift-quality.md. Built-in rules are deliberately NOT enabled —
# we want a low-noise, high-signal lint focused on the patterns that reviews
# keep flagging. Reach for built-in rules only if a review finding recurs that
# a custom rule cannot express precisely.
only_rules:
  - custom_rules

custom_rules:
  e1_try_silencing_critical_fs:
    name: "try? on critical filesystem op"
    regex: 'try\?\s+(FileManager\.default\.)?(createDirectory|moveItem|copyItem|setAttributes)\b'
    message: "try? swallows failure on a correctness-critical FS op — use do/catch (SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING §1)"
    severity: error

  e2_flock_lock_ex_without_nb:
    name: "flock LOCK_EX without LOCK_NB"
    regex: 'flock\([^)]*LOCK_EX(?![^)]*LOCK_NB)'
    message: "flock with LOCK_EX must be OR'd with LOCK_NB and wrapped in a timeout loop (SWIFT-CONCURRENCY / SWIFT-IO-ROBUSTNESS)"
    severity: error
    excluded:
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/.*\\.swift"

  w1_nonisolated_unsafe:
    name: "nonisolated(unsafe) in production"
    regex: 'nonisolated\(unsafe\)'
    excluded_match_kinds:
      - comment
      - doccomment
    message: "nonisolated(unsafe) bypasses Swift isolation — prefer actor-isolated storage or per-call allocation (SWIFT-CONCURRENCY)"
    severity: warning
    excluded:
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/.*\\.swift"

  w3_task_sleep_literal_in_tests:
    name: "Task.sleep with fixed literal in tests"
    regex: 'Task\.sleep\([^)]*[0-9]'
    message: "Task.sleep with a fixed literal is fragile under CI load — poll the observable state with the eventually() helper (SWIFT-TESTABILITY)"
    severity: warning
    included:
      - "AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/.*\\.swift"

  w4_unchecked_sendable:
    name: "@unchecked Sendable"
    regex: '@unchecked\s+Sendable'
    excluded_match_kinds:
      - comment
      - doccomment
    message: "@unchecked Sendable requires an adjacent justification comment describing the single-threaded contract (SWIFT-CONCURRENCY)"
    severity: warning

  w5_duration_literal_in_comment:
    name: "Duration literal embedded in comment"
    regex: '\b\d+\s*(hours?|days?|minutes?|seconds?|ms)\b'
    match_kinds:
      - comment
    message: "Comment embeds a bare `N unit` duration — lift to a named constant whose declaration site carries the rationale (SWIFT-TESTABILITY §Comment WHY)"
    severity: warning

content.content:
1	import Foundation
2	
3	public actor CodexStatusConnector: StatusConnector {
4	    nonisolated public let vendor: Vendor = .codex
5	
6	    private let logger: FileLogger
7	    private let session: URLSession
8	    private let endpointURLString: String
9	
10	    public static let defaultEndpoint = "https://status.openai.com/api/v2/incidents/unresolved.json"
11	
12	    public init(
13	        logger: FileLogger = Loggers.codex,
14	        session: URLSession = .shared,
15	        endpointURLString: String = CodexStatusConnector.defaultEndpoint
16	    ) {
17	        self.logger = logger
18	        self.session = session
19	        self.endpointURLString = endpointURLString
20	    }
21	
22	    public func fetchOutages() async throws -> [Outage] {
23	        guard let url = URL(string: endpointURLString) else {
24	            throw StatusConnectorError.invalidURL(rawValue: endpointURLString)
25	        }
26	        var request = URLRequest(url: url)
27	        request.timeoutInterval = CodexConstants.requestTimeoutSeconds
28	        request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Accept")
29	
30	        logger.log(.debug, "Fetching Codex status from \(endpointURLString)")
31	
32	        let data: Data
33	        let response: URLResponse
34	        do {
35	            (data, response) = try await session.data(for: request)
36	        } catch {
37	            logger.log(.warning, "Codex status endpoint network error: \(error)")
38	            throw StatusConnectorError.networkError(underlying: error, url: endpointURLString)
39	        }
40	
41	        let httpCode = (response as? HTTPURLResponse)?.statusCode ?? -1
42	        guard httpCode == 200 else {
43	            logger.log(.warning, "Codex status endpoint returned HTTP \(httpCode)")
44	            throw StatusConnectorError.unexpectedResponse(statusCode: httpCode, url: endpointURLString)
45	        }
46	
47	        let decoded: StatuspageIncidentsResponse
48	        do {
49	            decoded = try JSONDecoder().decode(StatuspageIncidentsResponse.self, from: data)
50	        } catch {
51	            logger.log(.error, "Codex status endpoint parse error: \(error)")
52	            throw StatusConnectorError.parseError(underlying: error, url: endpointURLString)
53	        }
54	
55	        let outages: [Outage] = decoded.incidents.compactMap { incident in
56	            // "none" impact means the incident is informational with no user-facing effect
57	            guard incident.impact != "none" else { return nil }
58	            let severity = OutageSeverity(rawValue: incident.impact)
59	            let href: URL?
60	            if let raw = incident.shortlink {
61	                href = URL(string: raw)
62	            } else {
63	                href = nil
64	            }
65	            return Outage(
66	                vendor: vendor,
67	                errorMessage: incident.name,
68	                severity: severity,
69	                since: incident.createdAt,
70	                href: href
71	            )
72	        }
73	        logger.log(.info, "Codex status: \(outages.count) active outage(s)")
74	        return outages
75	    }
76	
77	    // MARK: - DTOs
78	
79	    private struct StatuspageIncidentsResponse: Decodable {
80	        let incidents: [StatuspageIncident]
81	    }
82	
83	    private struct StatuspageIncident: Decodable {
84	        let name: String
85	        let impact: String
86	        let createdAt: ISODate
87	        let shortlink: String?
88	
89	        private enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
90	            case name
91	            case impact
92	            case createdAt = "created_at"
93	            case shortlink
94	        }
95	    }
96	}
97

content.subject: Étape 6 — Garde-fous: SwiftLint & tests de conformance
content.description: Règle SwiftLint bloquant les writes dans *CredentialLocator.swift. Règle SwiftLint imposant LoggingProxy pour tout payload-bearing log. Test de conformance contrat (no .unknown, ISO 8601 strict, branding asset présent, vendor doc dated).

content.subject: Étape 7 — Mode verbose vendor
content.description: Câbler env AI_TRACKER_VENDOR_DEBUG + clé Info.plist AITrackerVendorDebug. Wiring dans le layer logging pour scoper le .debug à un seul vendor.

content.subject: Étape 1 — Specs & docs vendor
content.description: Rédiger docs/VENDOR-PLUGIN-CONTRACT.md, docs/vendors/_TEMPLATE.md, docs/vendors/claude.md, docs/vendors/codex.md, docs/vendors/copilot.md. Mettre à jour CLAUDE.md lazy-loaded context.
content.activeForm: Rédaction des specs et docs vendor

content.subject: Étape 2 — Types pivots du framework
content.description: Créer VendorBundle, VendorBranding (value object), ActiveAccountMonitoring, CredentialLocator, PayloadSanitizing, LoggingProxy, VendorRegistry. Ajout pur sans toucher les call sites existants.

content.subject: Étape 3 — Migration Claude Code
content.description: Extraire ClaudeCredentialLocator depuis ClaudeCodeConnector. Créer ClaudeCodePlugin.bundle. Ajouter ClaudePayloadSanitizer + leakage test + fixture.

content.subject: Étape 4 — Migration Codex & Copilot
content.description: Renommer CodexAuth*→CodexCredentialLocator*, CopilotAuth*→CopilotCredentialLocator*. Créer CodexPlugin.bundle et CopilotCLIPlugin.bundle. Sanitizers + leakage tests + fixtures.

content.subject: Étape 5 — Bascule AppDelegate, branding, loggers
content.description: AppDelegate itère sur VendorRegistry au lieu de propriétés nommées. VendorBranding switch → registry lookup. Loggers: FileLoggers portés par les bundles.
